visualGoal: Make a brand-new MSFS 2024 pilot feel calm about the buy order: fly free first, then buy the ONE control that matches their airplane. Real, verified product proof on every recommended first buy (yoke, side-stick, console stick, throttle, pedals); no procedural art. The two most-cited beginner products (Logitech G PRO Yoke, Logitech G rudder pedals) are honestly text-only until real photos are sourced.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Logitech G PRO Flight Yoke System
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: The single most-recommended beginner GA buy ($179.99 official). Currently text-and-link only in Build 1 because no verified image. Highest-priority photo to source: this is the article's headline budget pick. Until sourced, its /flight/gear/logitech-g-pro-flight-yoke-system page does not build (gear route requires a verified image).
The standard budget rudder-pedal answer ($179.99 official). Text-and-link only in the rudder section until a real photo is sourced. Its gear page also does not build until an image exists.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Thrustmaster TCA Sky Yoke (verified .png exists but product is pre-launch)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Image IS in the verified set (.png). Referenced in a pro-tip and the verdict as a Q3 2026 watchlist console-yoke option, but deliberately NOT embedded as a gearpick because the product has not shipped and specs/price are pre-launch. Promote to an embedded gearpick once retail units + reviews confirm the specs.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic warm-lit home desk at dusk with cyan and amber cockpit glow, a clean GA sim setup: an empty desk-edge clamp mount left open for a real yoke product overlay, an Xbox-style gamepad resting beside it to imply the free starting point, curator Mac Donovan gesturing from gamepad toward the mount as a step up, no fake branded hardware, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, fake yoke, warped controls, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: What to buy first (and what to skip)
content: 1 One control matched to your airplane: yoke (GA) / side-stick (Airbus) / stick (Xbox)
3 Rudder pedals — only when a maneuver demands them
SKIP on day one: pedals, panels, head tracking
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center numbered ladder, large readable text, flight colors, real product photos as row markers for Codex overlay

infographicPrompts.title: Three beginner builds by budget
content: ~$130-180 Get airborne: Logitech G PRO Yoke / TCA Sidestick / VelocityOne
~$300 Committed GA: Honeycomb Alpha + basic quadrant
infographicPrompts.style: Three stacked price-tier cards, product-photo placeholders for Codex overlays, flight accent colors

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult flight-sim curator Mac Donovan seated at a tidy home cockpit desk, one hand resting on a GA yoke, calm mentor energy pointing at a small Cessna on screen, product overlay space beside him, practical not fantasy, 4:5 portrait.
